statement.markdown: Your task is to implement selection sort to sort an array of integers in ascending order. However, the current implementation contains a bug which prevents it from sorting correctly. Please identify and fix the bug.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided as an array of integers on separate lines.
statement.outputFormat: Output will be the sorted array of integers on separate lines.
statement.notes: The selection sort algorithm works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted part of the array and swapping it with the first unsorted element.

hints.text: Check the logic of the selection sort algorithm, where you are comparing and finding the minimum element from the unsorted part of the array.
Pay close attention to the swapping of elements, as this is where the bug lies in the current implementation.

editorial.title: Selection Sort
editorial.intuition: Selection sort is a simple sorting algorithm that works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted part of the array and swapping it with the first unsorted element.
editorial.optimalSolution: Selection sort works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted part and putting it at the beginning. The algorithm maintains two subarrays in a given array: the subarray which is already sorted, and the remaining subarray which is unsorted. In every iteration of selection sort, the minimum element (considering ascending order) from the unsorted subarray is picked and moved to the sorted subarray.
